nodes.description: The frontend is organized as one client-side workspace that switches between Notes, Inspect, Project, and Chat views while keeping shared state such as the active repo, open tabs, selected notes, query results, and console messages. Most product behavior is coordinated inside the `Home` component, which refreshes backend state, streams long-running runs, and assembles semantic context from notes and conversations before invoking AI workflows.
nodes.title: Workspace Shell

nodes.description: Users choose the working repository, see recent projects, inspect workspace readiness, and manage per-project conversation threads from the same product surface. The backend persists the active project profile and recent repositories, counts visible files to decide whether setup guidance should appear, and stores project-local metadata under `.konceptura`.
nodes.title: Project Workspace Setup

nodes.description: Indexing is the boundary that turns a repository into a queryable code graph by invoking the local `code-graph-rag` binary with `--update-graph` and optional `--clean`. The backend tracks one async index job at a time, records status in `backend/data/state.json`, and exposes both dry-run command previews and live status for the UI.
nodes.title: Repository Indexing

nodes.description: Graph inspection is centered on Memgraph, where the backend opens a Neo4j-compatible driver and translates records into JSON-safe structures. This layer provides symbol listing, ranked text search, relationship traversal, and a reconstructed structure tree, which makes the product behave like a semantic browser rather than a file-only explorer.
nodes.title: Code Graph Access

nodes.description: Before edits, the product can infer likely touch points by matching prompt tokens against graph symbols and then walking `CALLS` and `IMPORTS` edges in both directions. The result is a ranked impact summary with seed symbols, related symbols, and affected files, which is reused both as a user-facing inspect feature and as machine context for Codex runs.
nodes.title: Impact Analysis

nodes.description: The canvas is the editable knowledge layer for the current repository: users can create, move, edit, delete, and connect notes that represent responsibilities and architecture. Notes are stored per repo in `.konceptura/canvas.json`, deduplicated by title during generated imports, and rendered on a zoomable React Flow board that only shows edges around the current selection.
nodes.title: Architecture Notes Canvas

nodes.description: The product can ask Codex to inspect the repository and return a compact JSON architecture map in the same schema expected by the visual canvas. The backend validates generated nodes and edges, falls back to generic notes if parsing fails, and appends only new titles so generated maps can enrich an existing workspace instead of replacing it.
nodes.title: Generated Architecture Mapping

nodes.description: Planning, asking, and building all run through the local Codex CLI, with prompts augmented by selected note context, conversation history, and optional graph-derived impact hints. Build mode makes real repository edits, captures changed files by snapshot diffing, then regenerates architecture notes so the workspace reflects the new code state.
nodes.title: AI Planning And Build Loop

nodes.description: Each project keeps lightweight conversation history so planning and build runs can continue a thread instead of treating every request as isolated. The backend stores conversation documents under `.konceptura/conversations.json`, sorts them by last activity, and the frontend injects the most recent messages into AI requests as compact context blocks.
nodes.title: Conversation Memory

nodes.description: The workspace exposes `AGENTS.md` as a first-class project document so repository-specific operating rules can be read and updated inside the app. This gives the user a controllable policy layer that lives with the target repo and can shape future AI runs without changing application code.
nodes.title: Project Policy Documents

nodes.description: After edits, the backend can inspect git status, ask Codex for a concise conventional commit message suggestion, and create a real commit if the repo is dirty. This turns the workspace into a closed-loop editing surface that not only changes code but also packages the result for source control.
nodes.title: Commit Packaging

nodes.description: Runtime behavior is intentionally local-first: config comes from `konceptura.toml` plus environment overrides, with paths for Memgraph, the code-graph binary, the Codex binary, state files, and sample repositories. The top-level scripts and Docker compose file complete that boundary by starting Memgraph and the frontend/backend pair as one local stack.
nodes.title: Local Runtime Configuration
